title: Four Against Darkness - Deep Dive Part 2 - Base Game review
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zsYZHcSRvB8

Daniel (@dungeondive) delivers the deep dive proper, examining Four Against Darkness' base game with the enthusiasm of someone who genuinely loves the system while remaining critical of its presentation. He's unsparing about the book's structural flaws—rules scattered throughout, an awkwardly-placed FAQ that should've been integrated into the main text, and a general lack of logical flow that makes learning the game harder than it needs to be. Yet none of these criticisms diminish his genuine enjoyment of the actual gameplay.

The video meticulously walks through character creation (six races, multiple classes ranging from basic warriors to more complex wizards), the dungeon exploration mechanics, and the gloriously simple-yet-engaging combat system where you roll a d6, add bonuses, and compare to enemy levels. Particular attention goes to the clue system—arguably the game's most innovative mechanic—which lets players piece together secrets about dungeons and unlock meaningful benefits. He highlights how artists like Heather Shin, J.M. Woyak, and Jack Badashski elevate the book with genuinely impressive illustrations that often go unsung.

Daniel recommends the core bundle of base game plus Twisted Minions and Twisted Dungeons as the sweet entry point—under $20 total with months of entertainment potential. He's candid about the second edition coming later in 2021 and hopes it addresses the organizational issues plaguing the current version.

----

If you're on the fence about Four Against Darkness, what aspect sounds most appealing: the zen-like solo experience, character progression, or the creative dungeon-building?
